G: The seventh letter of the English alphabet.
game: An activity or contest with rules, played for fun or competition.
garbage: Waste material or rubbish to be thrown away.
garden: A piece of land where plants, flowers, or vegetables are grown.
gave: Past tense of 'give': handed something to someone.
geese: Plural of 'goose': large water birds related to ducks.
get: To obtain or receive something; to become.
ghost: The spirit of a dead person believed to appear to the living.
gift: Something given to someone as a present.
girl: A female child or young woman.
give: To hand something to someone or provide something for them.
glad: Feeling happy and pleased about something.
glass: A hard transparent material; a container for drinking.
glasses: A pair of lenses in frames worn on the eyes to aid vision.
go: To move from one place to another.
goal: The purpose you are trying to achieve; a point scored in a sport.
gold: A valuable yellow metal; the color of gold.
gone: Past participle of 'go': departed or no longer present.
good: Of high quality; producing a positive effect; morally correct.
got: Past tense of 'get': obtained or received.
gotten: Past participle of 'get': obtained or come to have.
grade: A mark given for schoolwork; a year level in school.
grammar: The rules that govern how words are combined in a language.
grandfather: The father of your father or mother.
grandmother: The mother of your father or mother.
grandparent: The parent of your parent; a grandmother or grandfather.
grape: A small round fruit that grows in clusters, used to make wine.
grass: The green plants that cover lawns and fields.
great: Of an unusually high quality; very large or important.
green: The color of leaves and grass; relating to the environment.
greet: To say hello or welcome someone when they arrive.
grew: Past tense of 'grow': became larger or more developed.
ground: The solid surface of the earth; the floor or soil.
group: A number of people or things placed together.
grow: To increase in size or amount; to cultivate plants.
grown: Past participle of 'grow': became larger or more mature.
guess: To give an answer without being sure it is correct.
guest: A person invited to visit or stay in someone's home or at an event.
guitar: A stringed musical instrument played by plucking or strumming.
guy: An informal word for a man or boy.
H: The eighth letter of the English alphabet.
habit: A regular practice or routine that is hard to give up.
had: Past tense of 'have': possessed or experienced something.
hair: The thin threads that grow from the skin of a person or animal.
haircut: The act of cutting someone's hair; a style of cutting hair.
half: One of two equal parts of something.
hall: A corridor or passageway in a building; a large room for events.
halves: Plural of 'half': two equal parts of something.
hamburger: A cooked beef patty served in a bun with toppings.
hand: The part of the body at the end of the arm, used for holding things.
handsome: Attractive and good-looking, typically used to describe a man.
happen: To take place or occur, often by chance.
happy: Feeling or showing pleasure and contentment.
hard: Solid and difficult to break; requiring much effort.
has: Third person singular present tense of 'have'.
hat: A covering worn on the head for protection or fashion.
have: To possess or own something; to experience something.
he: Third person singular pronoun used to refer to a male.
he'd: Short form of 'he would' or 'he had'.
head: The uppermost part of the body containing the brain; to lead.
headache: Pain or discomfort in the head.
health: The state of being free from illness; overall physical condition.
healthy: Having good health; promoting good health.
hear: To perceive sounds with the ears.
heard: Past tense and past participle of 'hear': perceived a sound.
heart: The organ that pumps blood through the body; the center of feelings.
heavy: Of great weight; difficult to lift or carry.
held: Past tense and past participle of 'hold': kept in a position.
hello: A greeting used when meeting someone or answering the phone.
help: To make it easier for someone to do something; assistance.
her: Belonging to or relating to a female person.
here: At, in, or to this place.
hide: To put something in a place where it cannot be found.
high: At a great distance above the ground; greater than normal.
hill: A raised area of land, smaller than a mountain.
him: The form of 'he' used as an object in a sentence.
his: Belonging to or relating to a male person.
history: The study of past events; the past record of something.
hobby: An activity done for pleasure in your free time.
hold: To keep something in your hand or arms; to contain.
hole: An opening or gap in something.
holiday: A period of time away from work or school; a public celebration day.
home: The place where you live; where you feel you belong.
hometown: The town or city where you were born or grew up.
homework: School tasks assigned to be done outside class time.
hope: A feeling that something good will happen; to wish for something.
horse: A large animal with four legs used for riding and carrying loads.
hospital: A place where sick or injured people receive medical care.
hot: Having a high temperature; feeling heat.
hotel: A building where travelers pay to stay overnight.
hour: A unit of time equal to 60 minutes.
house: A building in which people live as a family home.
how: In what way or manner; to what extent.
hungry: Feeling the need or desire to eat food.
hurry: To move or act quickly; to rush.
hurt: To cause pain to someone; to feel pain.
husband: The male partner in a marriage.
I: The ninth letter of the English alphabet.
i: The first person singular pronoun; used to refer to yourself.
i'd: Short form of 'I would' or 'I had'.
ice: Water that has frozen into a solid state.
idea: A thought or plan formed in the mind.
ideal: Most suitable or perfect for a particular purpose.
if: Used to introduce a condition or supposition.
imagine: To form a mental image of something not present.
important: Of great significance or value; having a major effect.
in: Located inside or within something; during a period of time.
information: Facts about something; knowledge provided or learned.
inside: Within the interior of something; indoors.
interested: Wanting to know or learn more about something.
interesting: Holding attention because it is unusual or exciting.
interview: A formal meeting where someone is asked questions.
interviewer: A person who asks questions in an interview.
into: Moving to the inside of something; becoming a different state.
introduce: To present someone to another person for the first time.
is: Third person singular present tense of 'be'.
island: A piece of land completely surrounded by water.
it: Used to refer to something already mentioned; not a person.
item: A single thing in a list or collection.
its: Belonging to or relating to the thing mentioned.
J: The tenth letter of the English alphabet.
jacket: A short coat worn over other clothing for warmth.
january: The first month of the year, with 31 days.
jeans: Trousers made of denim, often blue.
jet: A fast aircraft powered by jet engines; to travel quickly.
job: Regular paid work or employment; a task to be done.
join: To connect two things together; to become a member of a group.
judge: To form an opinion about something; a person who decides cases in court.
juice: The liquid that comes from fruit or vegetables.
july: The seventh month of the year, with 31 days.
jump: To push yourself off the ground into the air; to leap.
june: The sixth month of the year, with 30 days.
just: Only; exactly; very recently; fair and morally right.
K: The eleventh letter of the English alphabet.
keep: To continue to have or hold something; to remain in a state.
kept: Past tense and past participle of 'keep': continued to have.
key: A small metal device for locking and unlocking; most important.
kick: To strike something with your foot.
kid: Informal word for a child; a young goat.
kill: To cause the death of a living thing.
kind: Generous, gentle, and caring; a type or category of thing.
king: The male ruler of a country.
kiss: To touch someone with your lips as a sign of love or greeting.
kitchen: A room used for preparing and cooking food.
kite: A toy that flies in the air on a string, made of light material.
knee: The joint between the upper and lower leg.
knew: Past tense of 'know': had knowledge of something.
knife: A tool with a sharp blade used for cutting.
knives: Plural of 'knife': tools with sharp blades for cutting.
know: To have information about something in your mind; to be familiar with.
L: The twelfth letter of the English alphabet.
lady: A polite or formal word for a woman.
language: A system of communication using words and grammar.
large: Of more than usual size; big.
late: After the expected or usual time; near the end of the day.
later: At a time in the future; after this moment.
laugh: To make sounds showing amusement; to find something funny.
lazy: Not willing to work or use energy; preferring to rest.
leader: A person who guides or is in charge of a group.
leaf: A flat green part of a plant that grows on stems.
learn: To gain knowledge or a skill through study or experience.
leave: To go away from a place; to let something remain.
left: The direction opposite to right; what remains after others are gone.
leg: One of the limbs used for standing and walking.
lent: Past tense and past participle of 'lend': allowed temporary use of.
lesson: A period of time in which something is taught.
let: To allow someone to do something.
let's: Short form of 'let us'; used to make a suggestion.
letter: A written message sent to someone; a symbol of the alphabet.
library: A building where books and other materials can be borrowed.
life: The period between birth and death; the quality of being alive.
light: The energy that makes things visible; not heavy; a lamp.
like: To enjoy or have a positive feeling about something; similar to.
lily: A tall plant with large trumpet-shaped flowers.
line: A long thin mark; a row of people or things; a spoken part in a play.
lion: A large wild cat from Africa, known as the king of animals.
list: A number of items written one after another.
listen: To pay attention to sounds; to hear carefully.
little: Small in size or amount; young.
live: To have life and be alive; to make your home somewhere.
lives: Plural of 'life'; third person singular of 'live'.
living: Alive; the way in which one earns money or spends life.
lonely: Unhappy because you are alone or lack company.
long: Of great length; for a great amount of time.
look: To direct your eyes toward something; the appearance of something.
lot: A large number or amount; a specific piece of land.
love: A very strong feeling of affection; to care deeply for someone.
lovely: Attractive and pleasant; inspiring love or admiration.
luck: Success or good things that happen by chance.
lucky: Having good luck; benefiting from favorable circumstances.
lunch: A meal eaten in the middle of the day.
M: The thirteenth letter of the English alphabet.
machine: A device with moving parts that does work using power.
made: Past tense and past participle of 'make': created or produced.
magazine: A publication issued regularly with articles and pictures.
mail: Letters and packages sent through the postal system; to send by post.
make: To create or produce something; to cause something to happen.
many: A large number of; more than a few.
map: A flat diagram showing geographical features of an area.
march: The third month of the year with 31 days; to walk with regular steps.
match: A competition between two teams; to correspond or be equal.
matter: A situation or subject; to be important.
may: The fifth month of the year; used to express possibility or permission.
maybe: Perhaps; possibly; it might be the case.
me: The form of 'I' used as an object.
meal: An occasion when food is eaten; the food eaten at such a time.
mean: To intend or signify; unkind and unpleasant; the average.
meant: Past tense and past participle of 'mean': intended to express.
meat: The flesh of an animal used as food.
medicine: A substance taken to treat or prevent illness.
meet: To come together with someone for the first time or by arrangement.
meeting: An occasion when people gather together to discuss something.
memory: The ability to remember; something remembered from the past.
men: Plural of 'man': adult male human beings.
message: A communication sent from one person to another.
met: Past tense and past participle of 'meet': came together with someone.
middle: The point or position equally distant from the edges.
milk: A white liquid produced by cows and other mammals, used as food.
mind: The faculty for thinking and feeling; to object to something.
mine: Belonging to me; a place where minerals are extracted from the ground.
minute: A unit of time equal to 60 seconds; very small.
miss: To fail to hit or catch; to feel sad because someone is absent.
mobile: Able to move or be moved easily; a mobile phone.
moment: A very short period of time; a particular time.
monday: The first day of the working week.
money: Coins and notes used as a medium of exchange.
monkey: A primate with a long tail that lives in tropical regions.
month: One of the twelve periods into which a year is divided.
moon: The natural satellite of Earth that orbits it and reflects sunlight.
more: A greater amount or degree of something.
morning: The part of the day from when you wake up until midday.
most: The greatest amount or degree; nearly all of.
mountain: A large natural rise of the earth's surface, higher than a hill.
mouse: A small furry rodent with a long tail; a device for controlling a computer.
mouth: The opening in the face used for eating and speaking.
move: To change position; to go from one place to another.
movie: A film shown in a cinema or on screen.
much: A large amount of; to a great degree.
music: Vocal or instrumental sounds combined to create harmony and expression.
musician: A person who plays a musical instrument or sings professionally.
must: Used to express obligation or necessity.
mustn't: Short form of 'must not': strongly advised or required not to do.
N: The fourteenth letter of the English alphabet.
name: A word used to identify a person, place, or thing.
nationality: The status of belonging to a particular country.
near: At a short distance from; close to.
neck: The part of the body connecting the head to the trunk.
need: To require something as essential or important.
never: At no time; not ever; not at all.
new: Recently made, discovered, or introduced; not existing before.
news: Information about recent events in the world.
newspaper: A printed daily or weekly publication with news and articles.
nice: Pleasant or agreeable; kind and friendly.
night: The time from sunset to sunrise when it is dark.
no: Used to refuse or deny; not any.
nose: The part of the face used for breathing and smelling.
not: Used to make a statement negative.
note: A short written message; a musical sound; to observe carefully.
notebook: A small book with blank pages for writing notes.
november: The eleventh month of the year, with 30 days.
number: A mathematical unit used to count or measure.
nurse: A trained medical professional who cares for patients.
O: The fifteenth letter of the English alphabet.
october: The tenth month of the year, with 31 days.
of: Expressing a relationship between a part and a whole; belonging to.
off: Away from a place or position; not operating.
office: A room or building used for administrative or professional work.
officer: A person with authority, such as a police officer or military officer.
old: Having existed for a long time; advanced in age.
olympics: The international multi-sport games held every four years.
on: Touching or supported by a surface; about a topic; operating.
once: One single time; at some point in the past.
one: The number 1; a single thing or person.
only: No more than; just; sole or single.
open: Not closed; available; to cause something to be accessible.
opera: A dramatic performance set to music, with singers and an orchestra.
or: Used to connect alternatives or options.
orange: A round citrus fruit with bright orange skin; the color orange.
order: A request for something; the arrangement of things; to request.
other: Different from the one already mentioned; additional.
our: Belonging to us; relating to the speaker and others.
out: Away from the inside; not at home; visible.
outside: On the exterior; out of doors; beyond the limits of something.
over: Above; finished; more than a number; across.
own: Belonging to a specific person; to possess something.
P: The sixteenth letter of the English alphabet.
page: One side of a leaf in a book; a webpage.
paint: A liquid applied to a surface for color; to apply paint.
painting: A picture made with paint; the activity of applying paint.
pair: Two matching things; two people together.
palace: A large grand residence of a monarch or important person.
pants: Trousers; a garment covering the lower body and legs.
paper: Thin flat material for writing on or wrapping things.
paragraph: A section of writing that begins on a new line and covers one idea.
pardon: Forgiveness; asking someone to repeat what they said.
park: A public outdoor area with grass and trees; to leave a vehicle.
partner: A person who shares in an activity or business with another.
party: A social gathering for celebration; a political organization.
past: The period of time before the present; behind in position.
pay: To give money in exchange for goods or services; a salary.
peace: A state of freedom from conflict; calm and quiet.
pen: A writing instrument that uses ink.
pencil: A writing instrument with a graphite core inside wood.
people: Human beings; a group of persons.
period: A length of time; a mark used at the end of a sentence.
personal: Relating to a particular person; private.
pet: An animal kept at home for companionship.
phone: A telephone; to call someone by telephone.
photo: A photograph taken by a camera.
piano: A large keyboard musical instrument with strings hit by hammers.
pick: To select or choose; to pluck or remove something.
picnic: A meal eaten outdoors, usually packed in a basket.
picture: An image or painting; a photograph; a film.
piece: A part of something; a portion separated from a whole.
pig: A farm animal with a round body and short legs, kept for meat.
pink: A pale reddish color; a delicate shade between red and white.
pizza: A flat round bread base topped with tomato, cheese, and other toppings.
place: A particular position or location; to put something somewhere.
plan: An intention or arrangement for doing something; a drawing of a building.
plane: An aircraft that flies by engine power and wings.
play: To engage in activity for enjoyment; a dramatic work performed on stage.
player: A person who participates in a game or sport.
please: Used to make a request more polite; to make someone happy.
pleasure: A feeling of happiness and satisfaction.
pocket: A small bag sewn into clothing for carrying small items.
poem: A piece of writing that uses special language and rhythm to express ideas.
point: A specific location; a purpose or meaning; to indicate direction.
pollution: Harmful substances that contaminate the environment.
pool: A body of still water; a swimming pool.
poor: Having little money; of low quality.
pop: Popular music; a sudden sharp sound; a fizzy drink.
post: A system for sending mail; a job position; to publish online.
poster: A large printed picture or advertisement displayed on a wall.
potato: A starchy vegetable that grows underground, used in many dishes.
practice: Repeated exercise to improve a skill; the work of a professional.
pray: To speak to God or a higher power; to make a request.
present: A gift; the current time; to show or introduce something.
pretty: Attractive or pleasing to look at; to a considerable degree.
price: The amount of money for which something is sold.
prince: A male member of a royal family.
princess: A female member of a royal family.
problem: A situation that causes difficulty or requires a solution.
purple: A color made by mixing red and blue.
push: To press against something to move it away from you.
put: To place something in a particular position.
Q: The seventeenth letter of the English alphabet.
quarter: One of four equal parts; fifteen minutes.
question: A sentence asking for information or a response.
quickly: At a fast speed; with little delay.
quiet: Making little or no noise; calm and peaceful.
R: The eighteenth letter of the English alphabet.

radio: A device for receiving and broadcasting audio signals wirelessly.
rain: Water falling from clouds in drops; to fall as rain.
rainy: Having a lot of rain; wet weather.
ran: Past tense of 'run': moved quickly on foot.
rang: Past tense of 'ring': made a bell sound.
rat: A medium-sized rodent with a long tail, often seen as a pest.
read: To look at and understand written text.
reader: A person who reads; a book for learning to read.
reading: The activity of reading written text.
ready: Prepared and available for use or action.
real: Actually existing; not imaginary or pretend.
really: Actually; in truth; to a great degree.
reason: A cause or explanation for something.
